question_text: Which statement best explains why oil is used as a lubricant between moving metal parts in an engine?

options.label: A
options.text: Oil creates a slippery layer between surfaces so they do not directly touch, reducing friction and heat produced.

options.label: B
options.text: Oil makes metal harder and stronger, so friction cannot affect the metal parts.

options.label: C
options.text: Oil absorbs all friction, converting it into sound instead of heat.

options.label: D
options.text: Oil reduces the mass of the metal parts, which automatically decreases friction.

explanation: Lubrication works by creating a thin film between surfaces, preventing direct contact and greatly reducing friction. This is why engines require regular oil changes—without this protective layer, friction would damage the engine.
